metadata.title: Draft and Stress-Test a DAG Defense with Claude

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI ¬∑ Draft and Stress-Test a DAG Defense with Claude

beats.title: Kore cold open ‚Äî ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: I want to make my DAG defense survive an actual adversary, not just look tidy. Take a causal DAG I hand you and interrogate it edge by edge, the way a hostile reviewer would. Specifically: (1) for each arrow, state the mechanism that justifies its direction and name the strongest reversed-causation objection, (2) identify any missing common cause that would collapse my identification, and (3) rank the three edges most likely to break under scrutiny so I know where to gather evidence first.

props.command: I want to make my DAG defense survive an actual adversary, not just look tidy. Take a causal DAG I hand you and interrogate it edge by edge, the way a hostile reviewer would. Specifically: (1) for each arrow, state the mechanism that justifies its direction and name the strongest reversed-causation objection, (2) identify any missing common cause that would collapse my identification, and (3) rank the three edges most likely to break under scrutiny so I know where to gather evidence first.
props.segment: Draft and Stress-Test a DAG Defense

beats.narration_text: Here is why Draft and Stress-Test a DAG Defense with Claude belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level ‚Äî whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

props.command: I want to make my DAG defense survive an actual adversary, not just look tidy. Take a causal DAG I hand you and interrogate it edge by edge, the way a hostile reviewer would. Specifically: (1) for each arrow, state the mechanism that justifies its direction and name the strongest reversed-causation objection, (2) identify any missing common cause that would collapse my identification, and (3) rank the three edges most likely to break under scrutiny so I know where to gather evidence first.
props.segment: Draft and Stress-Test a DAG Defense

beats.narration: Claude drafts a professional-sounding DAG defense ‚Äî then you find the fabricated citation hiding in paragraph two.
A DAG defense is not about defending your assumptions ‚Äî it is about making them visible enough that someone else can attack them. Claude can draft the structure; the student does the fact-check.
We ask Claude to draft a three-part DAG defense for a healthcare DAG: justify each arrow, rank missing arrows by bias direction, and name two unmeasured confounders.

props.command:
claude "Draft a three-part DAG defense:
  DAG: treatment‚Üíoutcome, age‚Üíboth,
       severity‚Üítreatment, severity‚Üíoutcome
  (1) justify each arrow mechanistically
  (2) rank 3 missing arrows by bias direction
  (3) name 2 unmeasured confounders
  Use academic-register language."

beats.narration: Claude returns a three-part defense. Part one justifies each arrow. Part two ranks missing arrows. Part three names two confounders. One citation in part one ‚Äî open it. Does it exist?

props.code:
# dag_defense.md ‚Äî Claude output (excerpt)

## Part 1: Arrow Justifications
severity‚Üítreatment: high-severity patients
  receive more aggressive treatment
  (cf. Brookhart et al. 2006, NEJM) ‚Üê verify
age‚Üíoutcome: older patients have higher
  baseline mortality (mechanistic)
treatment‚Üíoutcome: direct biological
  mechanism via [pathway]

## Part 2: Missing Arrows (bias direction)
1. comorbidity‚Üíoutcome  (upward bias if omitted)
2. provider_skill‚Üítreatment (upward bias)
3. SES‚Üíboth              (downward bias)

## Part 3: Unmeasured Confounders
1. patient_preference (‚Üítreatment and outcome)
2. facility_quality   (‚Üítreatment and outcome)
props.title: dag_defense.md

beats.narration: The fact-check log: Brookhart et al. 2006 in NEJM ‚Äî verify this. The actual Brookhart citation is from Epidemiology 2006, not NEJM. Claude placed a real author in a wrong journal. The arrow justifications are mechanistic and defensible. The confounder bias directions are correct.
We ask Claude to rewrite the defense in plain language for a non-specialist audience and verify the structural claims are preserved without inventing new ones.

props.command:
claude "Rewrite the DAG defense in plain language
  for a non-specialist audience.
  Preserve all structural claims.
  Do not add new causal claims."

beats.narration: The plain-language version preserves the three-part structure. Sicker patients get more treatment ‚Äî that is the severity arrow. Older patients do worse ‚Äî the age arrow. Two things we did not measure that could affect both treatment choice and outcome ‚Äî the unmeasured confounders. No new claims added.

The DAG defense has three parts: justify each arrow mechanistically, rank the most dangerous missing arrows by bias direction, and name the unmeasured confounders. Claude drafts it ‚Äî you fact-check the citations and verify the bias-direction reasoning.
Next: propose and critique a confounder set ‚Äî find the two that are actually mediators, and the one that is a collider in disguise.
Like and subscribe for more causal reasoning with Claude.
